title: The temptation of Jesus part 8
scripture_text: ‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’” Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God, he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written: “‘He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’ Jesus answered him. ‘’It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ ’Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendour “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.” ‘Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.
devotional:
The third temptation.

Again this temptation may seem a bit strange or even unrelated to our every day living. I doubt any of us has ever faced a decision to become a king or not, we have never been offered kingdoms in exchange for Satanic worship. However, if we dig beneath the surface we will discover that we are inclined to think in ways that are similar to how the evil one thinks. For in his mind, position of power and honour equals authority and influence. God the Father brought Jesus into a wilderness and Satan offers him the Kingdoms of the world.

What a contrast, Satan is trying to bring Jesus out of the wilderness his Father has chosen for him.

The third temptation did not begin like the first two with the words, “If you are the Son of God ….” As we will see, the devil seems to have conceded for the moment that Jesus wanted to be an obedient son. So the thrust of the third temptation was this: What would Jesus the Son be willing to do to establish the Kingdom of God on earth?

This temptation exposes that the way the evil one thinks, is completely opposite to the way of the kingdom of God. Satan believed that position equalled authority and influence. Satan was suggesting ‘Jesus here is a wonderful opportunity for you to make a difference to this evil corrupt world. Think of the changes you could make, the laws you could pass, the evil you could crush if you were king right here right now.’

So often we can fall into the same way of thinking, we believe if we were in a more important position, or a more prominent situation we would make some real and lasting change. If we were ‘king for the day,’ this would be a different world.

Listen to these verses. Matthew 20:25-28

Jesus called them together and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their high officials exercise authority over them. Not so with you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant, and whoever wants to be first must be your slave— Just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”

For Jesus, the sphere of greatest influence was found in being a servant, and displaying a redeeming quality. We do not need to be in positions of prestige or authority to be an influence for God. Jesus knew that the way of servant-hood would be the most effective way to win peoples hearts and love. That is why he said, ‘’For it is written: 'Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.'

The problem with us is that we have a wrong understanding of authority. A person can be in a position of power and authority, but not be an influence of change and good. Many people in Christian circles hold positions of recognised authority, but their lives make no difference. In the New Testament, the Greek word for authority is exousia which carries the idea of freedom or being free. In other words biblical authority and influence is the expression of internal freedom. The greatest hindrance to real authority in a person is their lack of freedom. This causes us to ask the question, What/Who are we slaves to?

Are we slaves to our pride? Do we have a desperate need for recognition? Do we need a kingdom to massage our egos and make us feel good about ourselves? Satan will never offer us all the kingdoms of the world, but he will offer us some kind of kingdom that will appeal to our bondage and pride. Are we slaves to our ministry. Some people in the Christian context suffer from ministry addiction, their value and significance is rooted in their ministry. Their ministry is their kingdom. It is a place where they serve themselves instead of serving God

There are lots of things we can be slaves to, and our lack of freedom will mean that we will not be free enough to serve. Jesus came to earth to be a servant not a king because he knew that in the kingdom of God a servant is the person who has the most influence. When we are not free, and live in bondage to some negative power, we will be desperate for a position, a kingdom, and a crown.

Like the other temptations that Satan put before Jesus, there was more to this than what he is offering. He is offering Jesus a completely alternative way to live. He is offering Jesus an easier way to fulfil God’s purposes for his life. It was the will of the Father that Jesus would be King. See Psalm 24:7-10. Satan is saying ‘Jesus I am more than happy for you to be king of the whole earth, in fact you wont even have to suffer, all you have to do is stop living a life that is totally Father centred and bow and worship me.’

R.G.V. Tasker writes:

‘’To escape the way of the cross by being disobedient to the vocation of the suffering Servant despised and rejected by men, upon whom was to be laid the iniquity of us all, was Jesus’ greatest and most persistent temptation…. Jesus was in effect tempted to subscribe to the diabolical doctrine that the end justifies the means; that, so long as He obtained universal sovereignty in the end, it mattered not how that sovereignty was reached….The Gospel According to St. Matthew (Grand Rapids, Michigan: Wm. B. Eerdmans p54

None of us like suffering, we go to great lengths theologically and practically to avoid it, so Satan appeals to our aversion to pain and suffering. Suggesting ‘You deserve a crown not a cross, a throne not thorns, a palace not pain, life not death. Surely this is what God wants for you.’ We want pain free lives. In our thinking we do not have a contingency that will accommodate pain and suffering, we expect that our lives to be more about crowns than crosses.

Jesus would rather suffer and die to win peoples hearts, than be a cold distant monarch who had no intimacy with them. Jesus would rather suffer and die so he could bring the power of redeeming love to the world, than be a king that was able to control men’s behaviour or beliefs.

The thing that Satan fears most is the cross which symbolises the gospel. The crosses that we should be carrying are the ongoing expression of God’s redeeming work in the world. Our lives are meant to be instruments that God uses to display his redemptive love and grace to a dying world. Any refusal on our part to not take up our cross and cling to a crown of glory, position and prestige, will have a dreadful effect on the world in which we live. The evil one will appeal to our pride in order to prevent us from carrying our crosses for he knows that he has no power against the cross.

To carry you cross is to embrace a sacrificial life so you can express the same attitudes through living that Jesus expressed through His death.
prayer: Father help me through life to display the same attitudes that Jesus showed in his death.
html:
The temptation of Jesus part 8

Study Text: Matthew 4:1-1
‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’” Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God, he said, “throw yourself down. B. Eerdmans p54

Suggesting ‘You deserve a crown not a cross, a throne not thorns, a palace not pain, life not death. Surely this is what God wants for you.’ We want pain free lives. To carry you cross is to embrace a sacrificial life so you can express the same attitudes through living that Jesus expressed through His death.

Prayer: Father help me through life to display the same attitudes that Jesus showed in his death.
